data.title: Andrew Douglas Reiterates Hold Rating and Keeps $2765 Price Target After Solid Q1 but Limited Near-Term Catalysts
data.description:
Analyst Andrew Douglas from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on RHI Magnesita NV and keeping the price target at p2,765.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Douglas has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a solid first‑quarter performance against a mixed macroeconomic backdrop. Profitability improved meaningfully on an adjusted basis, helped by tight cost control and internal efficiency measures, while sales volumes in key segments were largely stable with only modest pricing gains and some weakness in certain regional steel markets.

Management has maintained its full‑year 2026 EBITA and leverage guidance, indicating confidence that further self‑help initiatives and existing programs will continue to support earnings, and current order intake suggests ongoing resilience. However, with net debt slightly higher, industrial demand only beginning to recover, and limited near‑term catalysts beyond execution of internal initiatives, Douglas sees the current valuation as fairly reflecting these dynamics, justifying an unchanged Hold rating and a steady price target of $2765.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Siltronic: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Solid Positioning, Manageable Leverage and Improving AI-Driven Demand Despite Near-Term Headwinds
data.description:
Constantin Hesse, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Buy rating on Siltronic. The associated price target is €70.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Constantin Hesse has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Siltronic’s operational performance and medium‑term outlook. He views Q1 as essentially in line despite minor misses on sales and EBITDA, especially given volatile D&A, and notes that AI‑related demand is steadily improving even if near‑term constraints at memory customers and elevated 200mm inventories temper the immediate growth picture.

Hesse also points to supportive elements such as a meaningful hedging gain on electricity‑linked oil components, confirmation of FY26 guidance, and a capital structure that remains manageable with net leverage around 2.8x EBITDA despite a rise in net debt. In his view, the recent share price strength, driven by robust signals from chip manufacturers, is justified by Siltronic’s solid positioning in the wafer market, and he expects the company to benefit as cyclical headwinds ease, which underpins his positive stance on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Hesse is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 11.2% and a 46.95% success rate.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
